methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $400M last post money.

data.narrative:
AiDash is a Series C-stage infrastructure and logistics software company founded by Abhishek Singh. The company has raised $91M+ to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $400M+. Comparable company analysis is constrained by the absence of direct public market peers and limited availability of recent private transaction data in the narrowly defined infrastructure-logistics software segment, limiting the depth of traditional multiples-based triangulation.

The primary applicable valuation method is stage-progression analysis, which anchors to AiDash's $400M last post-money valuation and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-Series D or later-stage progression. This methodology implies a forward valuation of $680M. The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $578M (low) to $782M (high), with $680M as the median. This range reflects typical venture-stage step-up mechanics rather than broad-based comparable company validation.

The primary uncertainty in this valuation stems from the absence of publicly traded comparables and insufficient private transaction precedent to benchmark multiples or validate the assumed 1.7x step-up factor for this specific sector and stage. The valuation range of $578M–$782M should therefore be treated as indicative of stage-typical progression rather than grounded in external market data. Future rounds or M&A activity would be required to substantiate these implied values against actual market clearing prices.
